finding.title: Render graph-context section in renderWorkerPrompt for external_analyzer_signal-tagged tasks
finding.summary: In src/audit/prompts/renderWorkerPrompt.ts, add a readGraphBundleForPrompt helper that reads graph_bundle.json via withFsRetry(() => readFile(path, 'utf8')) (never readJsonFile/readOptionalJsonFile), JSON.parse's it, and validates with GraphBundleSchema.strict().parse, all inside one try/catch degrading to undefined on any failure (ENOENT, malformed JSON, schema-invalid, or exhausted transient fs error). When task.pending_audit_tasks_path resolves an AuditTask carrying the current file_paths tagged 'external_analyzer_signal' and a graph bundle loads successfully, call collectGraphEdges(graphBundle) then buildPacketGraphContext(task.file_paths, flatEdges, graphBundle), map the camelCase result to the snake_case shape (entrypoints, key_edges, boundary_files, quality) mirroring reviewPackets.ts's buildPacket() rename, omit the quality field when task.file_paths.length <= 1, and append the rendered section as the last entries pushed into `lines` before `return lines.join('\n')` in both the agent and non-agent branches, after all existing trailing blocks. Use normalizeGraphPath for all path matching. No changes to src/shared/types/graph.ts or src/audit/orchestrator/nextStep.ts.
finding.concrete_change: In src/audit/prompts/renderWorkerPrompt.ts, add a readGraphBundleForPrompt helper that reads graph_bundle.json via withFsRetry(() => readFile(path, 'utf8')) (never readJsonFile/readOptionalJsonFile), JSON.parse's it, and validates with GraphBundleSchema.strict().parse, all inside one try/catch degrading to undefined on any failure (ENOENT, malformed JSON, schema-invalid, or exhausted transient fs error). When task.pending_audit_tasks_path resolves an AuditTask carrying the current file_paths tagged 'external_analyzer_signal' and a graph bundle loads successfully, call collectGraphEdges(graphBundle) then buildPacketGraphContext(task.file_paths, flatEdges, graphBundle), map the camelCase result to the snake_case shape (entrypoints, key_edges, boundary_files, quality) mirroring reviewPackets.ts's buildPacket() rename, omit the quality field when task.file_paths.length <= 1, and append the rendered section as the last entries pushed into `lines` before `return lines.join('\n')` in both the agent and non-agent branches, after all existing trailing blocks. Use normalizeGraphPath for all path matching. No changes to src/shared/types/graph.ts or src/audit/orchestrator/nextStep.ts.

finding.title: Register jscpd as a new external analyzer candidate
finding.summary: In src/audit/extractors/analyzers/candidates.ts, append a new jscpdCandidate: ExternalAnalyzerCandidate (id 'jscpd', runner 'npx', spec pinned to a jscpd major version, defaultRun: false, detect: detectNodeEcosystem, buildArgv producing JSON-reporter output to a deterministic per-process report path) after the existing four entries, and add parseJscpd alongside parseKnip/parseEslint/parseSemgrep that parses jscpd's raw JSON reporter output into the same generic finding-item array shape those parsers return, degrading to [] on malformed/empty/missing-'duplicates' input, and never itself calling normalizeGenericExternalResults. Append jscpdCandidate to EXTERNAL_ANALYZER_CANDIDATES (order-preserving) and re-export jscpdCandidate/parseJscpd. Do not modify acquisitionEngine.ts and do not add a production src/audit/adapters/jscpd.ts. Cross-run concurrent-edit sequencing risk to candidates.ts (OBL-analyzer-registry-proof-tool-fail-4) is out of scope for this run's diff -- no other in-flight module touches this file, confirmed by the module contract's own side_effects note.
finding.concrete_change: In src/audit/extractors/analyzers/candidates.ts, append a new jscpdCandidate: ExternalAnalyzerCandidate (id 'jscpd', runner 'npx', spec pinned to a jscpd major version, defaultRun: false, detect: detectNodeEcosystem, buildArgv producing JSON-reporter output to a deterministic per-process report path) after the existing four entries, and add parseJscpd alongside parseKnip/parseEslint/parseSemgrep that parses jscpd's raw JSON reporter output into the same generic finding-item array shape those parsers return, degrading to [] on malformed/empty/missing-'duplicates' input, and never itself calling normalizeGenericExternalResults. Append jscpdCandidate to EXTERNAL_ANALYZER_CANDIDATES (order-preserving) and re-export jscpdCandidate/parseJscpd. Do not modify acquisitionEngine.ts and do not add a production src/audit/adapters/jscpd.ts. Cross-run concurrent-edit sequencing risk to candidates.ts (OBL-analyzer-registry-proof-tool-fail-4) is out of scope for this run's diff -- no other in-flight module touches this file, confirmed by the module contract's own side_effects note.

finding.title: Record consent-gate-for-proposed-analyzers confirmation and defer the LLM-proposal-channel gap to the backlog
finding.summary: No source change. Add a docs/backlog.md entry under 'Open bugs / frictions' recording: (1) admitSpawn already gates every defaultRun:false candidate (including CP-NODE-2's jscpd) behind a non-empty consentToken -- confirmed, no gap; (2) there is no runtime path for an LLM to propose a brand-new analyzer id beyond the static EXTERNAL_ANALYZER_CANDIDATES array -- explicitly out of scope this round, tracked as a forward item; (3) the latent (not exercised) hazard that SessionConfig's persisted schema does not structurally strip external_acquisition.consent_token, should a future proposal-channel writer ever touch that field.
finding.concrete_change: No source change. Add a docs/backlog.md entry under 'Open bugs / frictions' recording: (1) admitSpawn already gates every defaultRun:false candidate (including CP-NODE-2's jscpd) behind a non-empty consentToken -- confirmed, no gap; (2) there is no runtime path for an LLM to propose a brand-new analyzer id beyond the static EXTERNAL_ANALYZER_CANDIDATES array -- explicitly out of scope this round, tracked as a forward item; (3) the latent (not exercised) hazard that SessionConfig's persisted schema does not structurally strip external_acquisition.consent_token, should a future proposal-channel writer ever touch that field.
